details: Q1 (Product vs SKU aggregate): answered in FR-001 in section 4 — Product is the aggregate root; SKU is a child entity
Q2 (Price on SKU or separate VO): answered in FR-004 in section 4 — Money VO on SKU, single-currency RUB only
Q3 (Status transitions): answered in FR-002 in section 4 — full allowed-transition table with backward-transition rules
Q4 (Multi-category): deferred in section 2 NOT included; carried to section 9 open question 1 with options and recommendation
Q5 (Soft-delete vs ARCHIVED): answered in FR-001 (deleted_at for soft-delete) and section 5 Product entity (both fields coexist)
Q6 (Optimistic locking at domain level): answered in FR-005 in section 4 — version field at domain level, optional on updates
Q7 (EAV validation method): answered in FR-003 — structured storage is prerequisite; completeness validator deferred per section 2 and section 9 tech Q3/Q4
Q8 (Supplier as domain entity): addressed in section 2 deferred list with activation trigger; section 3 supplier scenario explains FK-only approach
Q9 (variant_hash algorithm): answered in FR-006 — SHA-256 stdlib, includes product_id; nuance carried to section 9 technical Q2
Q10 (Domain events): addressed in section 2 deferred list with downstream-system activation trigger; section 10 Future tier

ADAPT variant_hash_algorithm: FR-006 with Simplification note, section 2 P1 inclusion, Appendix row — all 3 present

admin (catalog manager): complete scenario block in section 3 with 6-step happy path, 3 alternative scenarios, 4 edge cases
customer (storefront consumer): scenario block in section 3 with 4-step happy path, 2 alternatives, 1 edge case; customer-facing read endpoints are intentionally deferred to v1.1 per scope decision
supplier (product owner): minimal but complete scenario block in section 3; correctly thin because supplier is a referenced FK-only entity in v1.0 per the defer decision
FR-001 (P0): 6 ACs — all follow When [actor] does X the system Y format with concrete conditions and outcomes
FR-002 (P0): 7 ACs — each covering a specific status transition with explicit allow/reject and error details
FR-003 (P0): 6 ACs — each covering a distinct attribute-management operation with observable outcomes
FR-004 (P1): 4 ACs — cover creation, validation rule (compare > price), computed range, and update cascade
FR-005 (P1): 4 ACs — cover version in read response, successful update with version match, 409 on mismatch, and omission behavior
FR-006 (P1): 4 ACs — cover duplicate rejection, per-product scope, different-combination allowance, and update-to-duplicate rejection
Included P0 section (3 items) and P1 section (3 items) — both non-empty with activation rationale
NOT included section has two distinct subsections: Deferred (6 items with activation triggers) and Skipped (4 items with reasons)
Anti-patterns section is an additional strength: closes competitive-report feedback loop explicitly

Every included item maps to a FR: product/variant management -> FR-001, lifecycle -> FR-002, structured attributes -> FR-003, pricing -> FR-004, concurrent editing -> FR-005, variant uniqueness -> FR-006
No item appears in both included and NOT included lists

details.metric: KPI 3 — Average time to create and publish a product
details.issue: The Method column states 'Measure timestamp difference between ProductCreated and status=PUBLISHED'. ProductCreated is an explicitly deferred domain event (section 2 NOT included, section 10 Future). No domain event infrastructure exists in MVP, making this measurement method unavailable at v1.0 launch.

uncovered_requirements: FR-001 covers full CRUD for products; a product list endpoint (GET /api/v1/catalog/products) is absent from the section 6 Product operations table while the Admin interface subsection of section 6 explicitly mentions the product list view as a supported admin workflow

details.issue: GET /api/v1/catalog/products (paginated product list) is missing from the section 6 API endpoint table
details.context: The Product operations table in section 6 defines 5 endpoints (POST, GET/{id}, PUT/{id}, DELETE/{id}, PATCH/{id}/status) but does not include a list endpoint. The Admin interface paragraph in the same section says the API supports a Product list view. Section 2 correctly defers full attribute-filtered search to v1.1 read models, but a basic paginated list is required for the admin workflow in MVP.

revision_instructions.issue: KPI 3 measurement method references the ProductCreated domain event, which is explicitly deferred to the Future phase in section 10. The method is not executable in MVP because no domain event infrastructure exists at v1.0.
revision_instructions.section: section 8, Primary KPIs table — row 'Average time to create and publish a product', Method column
revision_instructions.fix_description: Replace the Method cell value from 'Measure timestamp difference between ProductCreated and status=PUBLISHED' with 'Measure timestamp difference between the product record created_at field and the timestamp when status is set to PUBLISHED (both fields are available on the Product ORM without requiring domain events)'.

revision_instructions.issue: FR-001 covers full product CRUD and the Admin interface paragraph in section 6 references a product list view, but no GET /api/v1/catalog/products list endpoint row is defined in the section 6 Product operations table.
revision_instructions.fix_description: Add a row to the Product operations table: Method=GET | Endpoint=/api/v1/catalog/products | Description='List products with cursor-based pagination (limit/after); optional filter by status and brand_id for MVP; full attribute filtering deferred to v1.1 optimized read models' | Priority=P0.

strengths: All 10 context-brief key questions are explicitly answered or deliberately deferred with documented activation triggers — no question is silently ignored or left unresolved.

Acceptance criteria across all six functional requirements are specific and fully testable: every criterion follows a concrete 'When [actor] does X, the system Y' format with no vague language from the banned list.
The decision log appendix achieves complete traceability: all 26 enhancement-plan patterns (3 adopt, 3 adapt, 6 defer, 9 skip, 5 anti-patterns) appear with decision, priority, and justification.

The anti-patterns section in scope is an outstanding addition: each of the 5 competitive-report anti-patterns is explicitly named and countered with the concrete approach taken in this PRD, closing the loop from competitive research.

The section 10 roadmap correctly sequences all nine declared dependencies — Product aggregate root ships before FSM, Money VO, optimistic locking, and variant hash build on it in v1.1, while completeness validation and read models correctly wait for their prerequisites in the Future tier.
